description: Ketubba. Location: Cairo. Dated: Friday, 3 Ṭevet 5581 AM = 8 December 1820 CE. Bride: Sūsār (or Sūkār?) bt. Yehuda Nabālī. Groom: Moshe b. Yaʿaqov Ḥabīb. Dowry is in medin currency. Witnessed by Ḥayyim Garyani (גריאני) and Meʾir Ben-Naʿim who signed on behalf of the groom and likely as scribe/witness in the lower right corner of the document (an identical ornamental ligature from the bottom of his seal is extant). The heading of the ketubba evokes Ruth 4:11. (ASE/MCD with information in part from CUDL.)
